title: Samosa

seo.title: Authentic Pakistani Samosa Recipe - Easy & Crispy!
seo.metaDescription: Learn to make the best authentic Pakistani Samosa recipe at home! Our easy, step-by-step guide ensures crispy, flavorful Samosas perfect for any occasion.
h2Headings: Ingredients You'll Need for Pakistani Samosa
How to Make Samosa: Step-by-Step Instructions
seo.imageAlt: Plate of golden brown, crispy authentic Pakistani Samosas with mint chutney
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy these delicious, homemade Pakistani Samosas – perfect for appetizers or a snack!

description: Samosas are a beloved Pakistani snack, featuring a crispy, flaky pastry shell filled with a savory mixture of spiced potatoes and peas. These deep-fried pockets of deliciousness are perfect for tea time, festive occasions, or any time you crave a flavorful treat. My recipe guides you to create perfectly golden and aromatic samosas at home.

instructions.title: Prepare the Samosa Dough
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine all-purpose flour, 1 teaspoon salt, and carom seeds if using. Add the melted ghee or oil and rub it into the flour until it resembles breadcrumbs. Gradually add water, kneading until a firm, smooth dough forms. Cover with a damp cloth and let it rest for at least 20 minutes.

instructions.title: Cook the Samosa Filling
instructions.text: Heat 2 tablespoons of oil in a pan over medium heat. Add chopped onions and saute until translucent. Add ginger-garlic paste and green chilies, cooking for another minute until fragrant. Stir in cumin powder, coriander powder, red chili powder, and turmeric powder. Cook for 30 seconds, then add the mashed potatoes and green peas. Mix well, season with salt, garam masala, and amchur powder (if using). Cook for 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the flavors meld. Remove from heat and stir in fresh coriander. Let the filling cool completely.

instructions.title: Roll and Shape the Samosas
instructions.text: Divide the rested dough into 8-10 equal portions. Roll each portion into an oval shape, about 6-7 inches long and 4-5 inches wide. Cut each oval in half lengthwise. Take one half, moisten the straight edge with a little water, and form it into a cone shape, pressing the seam firmly to seal.

instructions.title: Fill the Samosa Cones
instructions.text: Hold the cone in your hand and spoon about 1-2 tablespoons of the cooled potato filling inside. Do not overfill. Moisten the open edges of the cone with water. Press the edges together firmly to seal the samosa, creating a triangular shape. You can create a small pleat on one side for a classic look.

instructions.title: Deep Fry the Samosas
instructions.text: Heat oil for deep frying in a karahi or deep pot over medium-low heat. The oil should not be too hot, or the samosas will brown too quickly on the outside and remain raw inside. Carefully slide 3-4 samosas into the hot oil. Fry them on medium-low heat, turning occasionally, until they are golden brown and crispy on all sides. This usually takes about 8-10 minutes per batch.

instructions.title: Serve Hot
instructions.text: Once fried, remove the samosas with a slotted spoon and place them on a plate lined with paper towels to drain excess oil. Serve the hot, crispy samosas immediately with mint chutney, tamarind chutney, or ketchup. Enjoy these delightful Pakistani appetizers!

tips: For the crispiest samosa pastry, ensure your dough is firm and not too soft. The fat (ghee or oil) rubbed into the flour creates the flaky texture.
Always let your filling cool completely before filling the samosas. Hot filling will make the dough soggy and difficult to seal, leading to breakage during frying.
Fry samosas on medium-low heat. This allows the pastry to cook through and become golden brown and crisp without burning, ensuring a perfectly cooked samosa inside and out.
article: Samosa is a beloved Pakistani Appetizers dish enjoyed across Pakistan. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
